title: [$250] HybridApp - Switch to Expensify Classic silently does nothing on mobile when NVP_TRY_NEW_DOT is populated before the SESSION callback fires

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
**Version Number:** v9.3.60-17 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Likely (introduced in [#87101](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87101), deployed 2026-04-08) **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** jules@expensify.com **Issue reported by:** @Julesssss (noticed while verifying [#88182](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/88182) fix) ## Action Performed 1. Open the NewDot app on HybridApp (iOS or Android Native) 2. Do not start a GPS trip 3. Navigate to Account -> Troubleshoot 4. Tap "Switch to Expensify Classic" ## Expected Result App hands off to Expensify Classic. `NVP_TRY_NEW_DOT.classicRedirect.dismissed` flips to `true` server-side. ## Actual Result Nothing happens. User stays in NewDot. `NVP_TRY_NEW_DOT` is unchanged server-side: ```json { "classicRedirect": { "dismissed": false, "timestamp": "2026-04-07T15:20:32.352Z" }, "distance": { "enabled": "true" }, "smartScan": { "enabled": "true" }, "tappedTryNewExpensifyButton": { "timestamp": "2026-04-07T15:20:32.352Z" } } ``` Web is unaffected (web does not call `closeReactNativeApp` for this flow). ## Root Cause In [`src/libs/actions/HybridApp/index.ts:54-67`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/actions/HybridApp/index.ts#L54-L67), the SESSION callback unconditionally resets module-level `currentTryNewDot = undefined` and `isLoadingTryNewDot = true` on the first transition from `undefined` to the user's `accountID`: ```ts Onyx.connectWithoutView({ key: ONYXKEYS.SESSION, callback: (session) => { const nextSessionAccountID = getSessionAccountID(session); if (nextSessionAccountID === currentSessionAccountID) return; currentSessionAccountID = nextSessionAccountID; currentTryNewDot = undefined; hasReceivedTryNewDotUpdate = false; isLoadingTryNewDot = nextSessionAccountID !== undefined || isLoadingApp !== false; }, }); ``` If the NVP_TRY_NEW_DOT callback fires **before** the SESSION callback on app start (common on mobile), `currentTryNewDot` is set, then immediately blanked. Because the Onyx NVP value itself does not change, the NVP callback never re-fires to repopulate it. The module is permanently stuck with `isLoadingTryNewDot = true`. Any subsequent call to `closeReactNativeApp({shouldSetNVP: true})` hits the loading-state branch of `shouldBlockOldAppExit` at [`index.ts:87`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/actions/HybridApp/index.ts#L87) and silently returns. The Troubleshoot button is still rendered because the component-level `useOnyx` sees the NVP value fine — this is the desync. Introduced by [#87101](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87101) (merged 2026-04-08). ## Proposed Fix Skip the module-level reset on the initial `undefined -> accountID` transition. Only blank `currentTryNewDot` when the user actually changes (logout/login or cross-user switch): ```ts if (nextSessionAccountID === currentSessionAccountID) return; const isInitialSessionLoad = currentSessionAccountID === undefined; currentSessionAccountID = nextSessionAccountID; if (isInitialSessionLoad) return; currentTryNewDot = undefined; hasReceivedTryNewDotUpdate = false; isLoadingTryNewDot = nextSessionAccountID !== undefined || isLoadingApp !== false; ``` Existing tests that codify the re-block-on-session-switch behavior (`tests/unit/HybridAppActionsTest.ts:118-156`) continue to pass because they test a real accountID change, not the initial load. ## Workaround None. Users on mobile who land in this state cannot switch to Classic until they fully sign out and sign back in (which may also fail on first session). Web is unaffected. ## Platforms - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Related - [#88182](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/88182) - Deploy blocker for the GPS-specific manifestation of this silent return - [#88237](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88237) - Fix for #88182, scoped to the GPS path only [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abzokhattab

  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022045263129318285513
  • Upwork Job ID: 2045263129318285513
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-04-17

title: [$250] Add new concierge message after 30 days of broken card connection

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
@JmillsExpensify and I have been discussing ways to improve the broken card connection experience. We realized recently that many customers leave broken connections indefinitely. We suspect this means they aren't aware that removing a card with a broken connection is a valid resolution path when it's no longer in use, expired, etc. We already immediately send a concierge message in the #admins room when a company card connection breaks OR in the concierge DM when a personal card connection breaks. Now, we'd like to add an additional message sent in the same respective place for company cards and personal cards if the connection remains broken for 30+ days. ### Broken company cards message - Concierge message sent in #admins room 30 days after company card feed connection breaks. - Draft copy: >The %feed name% connection has been broken for 30 days. [Log into your bank](link to workspace company cards page) to fix it, or [remove the connection](link to workspace company cards settings page) if it's no longer in use. We won't delete any submitted expenses if you remove it. ### Broken personal card message - Concierge message sent in concierge DM 30 days after personal card connection breaks. - Draft copy: >Your %bank - card number% connection has been broken for 30 days. [Log into your bank](link to personal card details) to fix it, or [remove the card](link to personal card details) if it's no longer in use. We won't delete any submitted expenses if you remove it.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @truph01

title: Use a larger button for "X selected" on the Reports page to aid discoverability

labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly

body:
We're finding that a lot of folks select rows (expenses, reports, etc) from the Reports page and miss the green button that allows them to take bulk actions. As a first step, we'd like to experiment with simply making this button bigger to see if that aids in discoverability. cc @Expensify/design @Expensify/product

title: [$250] Reports page - Custom report title briefly shows "Chat Report" when clicking a report row

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
**Version Number:** N/A **Reproducible in staging?:** Unknown **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Internal **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1776378578696719 ## Action Performed: 1. Open New Expensify (Web) 2. Go to the Reports page 3. Set up a report with a custom report title (via title formula) 4. Click on a report row to open the report ## Expected Result: The report title in the title column should remain as the custom report title throughout the transition. ## Actual Result: When clicking on a report row, the custom report title is briefly changed to "Chat Report" before reverting back to the correct custom title once the report loads. This appears to be related to an optimistic update that temporarily replaces the resolved report name with a default/fallback value. ## Workaround: None known — this is a visual flicker only and does not affect functionality. ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Notes A similar but distinct issue existed for the chat search/switcher context (Expensify/App#84140), which was closed as not reproducible. This issue is specific to the Reports page and likely involves the custom report title formula not being applied during the optimistic update when a report row is clicked.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @thesahindia

title: [$250] [LHN] "Fix" action badge incorrectly appears on both workspace chat and individual expense report

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

labels.name: Hot Pick
labels.description: Ready for an engineer to pick up and run with

body:
## Bug: "Fix" action badge appears on both workspace chat and individual expense report in LHN When there are violations on forwarded receipts (triggering the DEW flow), the `Fix` action badge/pill appears on **both** the workspace chat and the individual expense report in the LHN. It should only appear on the workspace chat, not the individual report. ### Expected behavior The `Fix` action badge should only show on the workspace chat in the LHN, not on the individual expense report. ### Actual behavior Both the workspace chat and the individual expense report display the `Fix` icon/pill in the LHN. Additionally: - The expense report appears to be pinned in the LHN - This seems correlated with the DEW error message: _"This report can't be submitted. Please review the comments to resolve."_ — whenever that red text appears in the header, the double `Fix` pills also appear in the LHN ### How to reproduce No reliable repro steps yet. The reporter observed this after forwarding receipts that resulted in violations. Onyx state was shared in the Slack thread for debugging. ### Related context - This may be related to the DEW (Daily Expense Workflow) handling of violations - Possibly related to Expensify/App#86287 (phantom GBR on workspace chat) **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1775659495167829
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @linhvovan29546

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.60-6 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** N/A - new feature, doesn't exist in prod **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/86297 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** ibellicotest+2250@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Motorola MotoG60 / Android 12 (Hybrid app) - Windows 11 / Chrome **App Component:** Search ## Action Performed: 1. Open the staging.new.expensify.com website. 2. Sign up with a new account. 3. Complete onboarding selecting "Track and budget expenses" option. 4. Navigate to workspace chat. 5. Create a manual expense but don´t open report. 6. Navigate to "Reports" > "Expenses" 7. Click on "Display" > "Limit Results" 8. Set the limit to "1" and apply it. 9. Once redirected to results, note that "Status" field on just created expense is displayed empty. 10. Try to open the expense report. 11. Note that no action is triggered. 12. Click on "Display" > "Limit Results" > "Reset" 13. Open and close the expense report. 14. Click on "Display" > "Limit Results" one more time. 15. Set the limit to "1" again and apply it. 16. Note that now, status on expense preview is visible and report can be opened. ## Expected Result: After applying the "Limit Results" filter, user should be able to open the expenses on results, and preview of expenses should correctly display the status. ## Actual Result: After applying the "Limit Results" filter, expense displayed on results can´t be opened and "Status" field is displayed empty. This issue reproduces if expense report wasn´t opened before. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a226b160-2081-4076-85d3-7443c17d550a [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.60-6 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87704 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Pull Request QA execution **Device used:** Windows 11 / Chrome, Samsung Galaxy A52 / Android 14, iPhone 13 / iOS 18.7.1 **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: Preconditions: logged in with a user that at least has one workspace and invited a member to the workspace set the member as approver of admin's expense in advanced workflow Go to Workspace > Workflows and ensure: Scheduled Submit is enabled and Approval workflow is configured with Manager as the approver 1. As Employee, create at least 2 expenses on the workspace 2. Submit the expense report (or wait for Scheduled Submit to auto-submit it) 3. As Manager, open the submitted expense report 4. Reject a single expense -- go to OldDot staging (staging.expensify.com), navigate to the submitted report approve screen, and click the reject (X) button on one individual transaction. Add an optional comment like "Missing receipt" 5. As member, delete the report is not rejected 6. As member open selfDM ## Expected Result: Rejected expense that is already deleted should be moved to self DM ## Actual Result: Deleted rejected expense is not moved to Self DM ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/eedeff33-9f47-4b44-be4e-5e916741ccce [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
